text: Artificial intelligence is becoming a bigger part of our daily lives, but its use raises important ethical questions. One major concern is bias: if AI learns from data that reflects unfair human prejudices, it may make biased decisions. This threatens fairness, especially in areas like hiring or lending. Another issue is privacy—AI systems often collect large amounts of personal data, which must be protected. To build trust, companies need transparency, meaning they should explain how their AI works and why it makes certain choices. Ultimately, responsible AI development requires balancing innovation with these values to avoid harming individuals or society.
